page: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include a dedicated GPU, and local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, indicating that primary storage needs must be met via external network storage. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests scoring in the middle 50%, likely influenced by the shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is highly competitive; the Passmark memory score and large-block memory write bandwidth of 195.06 GB/sec rank in the top 10%. Single-threaded data processing is also highly efficient, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reaching top-tier performance levels. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 52.68 tokens/sec on a medium 7B LLM.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high density of memory and vCPUs within a shared-resource model, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity and high-throughput thread counts without needing dedicated, isolated CPU execution. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a performance tradeoff, as multi-core scaling and database operations perform in the average tier compared to dedicated instances. This server is suited for static web serving, memory-intensive caching layers like Redis, single-threaded data processing pipelines, and lightweight LLM inference workloads.
description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. Lacking a dedicated GPU and featuring only a 1 GB network-attached local storage volume, this instance relies on external network storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU and top-tier memory write bandwidth, though multi-core CPU scaling and database operations fall into the average tier due to the shared CPU allocation. Single-threaded compression and lightweight LLM text generation perform exceptionally well. This hardware profile is suited for static web serving, high-throughput Redis caching, and memory-intensive data processing workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and network-attached storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g is a general-purpose server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth for web serving and caching.
tagline: A high-capacity shared-CPU instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for high-throughput caching and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory.
Top-tier memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance.
Network-attached storage architecture with 1 GB local boot volume.
Strong single-core performance paired with average multi-core scaling.
Well-suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and lightweight LLM inference.
